terms.definition: An absent or unrecorded entry in a dataset feature, often represented as NaN, null, blank, or a sentinel placeholder.
Missing Completely At Random: the probability a value is missing does not depend on observed or unobserved data.
Missing At Random: missingness depends only on observed variables, not on the unobserved missing value itself.
Missing Not At Random: the probability of missingness is related to the value that would have been observed.
Replacing missing entries with substituted values—mean, median, model prediction, or forward-fill—so downstream steps receive complete inputs.
Dropping entire rows that contain any missing value in the columns used for modeling; also called complete-case analysis.
A scikit-learn transformer that learns per-column fill values (mean, median, mode, or constant) on training data and applies them at transform time.
